title: Fragment: The Sword Knows
description: Found in the forest clearing where the Arbiter fell. A piece of steel holds the shape of a blade that no longer exists. Brief, precise — the way she spoke.

lines: The clearing was still. The corruption at its edges had retreated slightly — not gone, but pulled back, as if uncertain what to do with a space that had recently been used for something honest.
Where the Arbiter had knelt, the grass was pressed flat in a precise shape: two points of contact, exactly even, the impression of something that had gone down with intention.
In the center of the impression, a piece of steel. Not a weapon. The shape of one — the suggestion of an edge, the memory of weight — but flattened into something that could be held without cutting.

steps.text: Picking it up, the voice came — not from the steel, exactly, but from the space the Arbiter had occupied. Very direct. As if directness were the only courtesy.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: I said the sword knows. I meant it, and I didn't know what I meant by it.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: Some things are true before you understand them.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: The sword knows that you were real. That's the only kind of knowing that matters — the kind where you have to be present for it.

lines.speaker: Arbiter
lines.text: You can't know that way from a distance.

steps.text: The piece of steel held its shape. Whatever the Arbiter had measured in the fight — whatever final assessment she had reached — it had been pressed into the metal at the moment she decided to stop.
It had the feeling of a verdict that came out in your favor, delivered by someone whose verdicts were worth receiving.

steps.name: Arbiter's Recognition
steps.description: A piece of steel that still holds the shape of a blade. The Arbiter's final assessment, material form. Grants +10% critical hit rate on attacks made during the first round of combat.
